text:
Admission Date:  2013-04-28       Discharge Date:  1990-03-08


Service:

ADDENDUM:  Discharge date was 1990-03-08.

HOSPITAL COURSE:

Amendment to the last two paragraphs of Upper
Gastrointestinal Bleed; the second-to-last paragraph should
read:

By the time of discharge, the patient was on a proton pump
inhibitor (Protonix 40 mg p.o. b.i.d.), in addition to
aspirin 81 mg p.o. q.d., and Plavix.  Plavix and low-dose
aspirin were started in light of the patient's stable
hematocrit.  Both should be discontinued if any signs of
further gastrointestinal bleed.  At present, they are
cardioprotective and should be kept.  The patient's
hematocrit remained steady prior to discharge.  His discharge
hematocrit was 36.1.  A serum Helicobacter pylori was sent
and was negative.

Addendum to page 4 of Hospital Course number 2; last
paragraph prior to Discharge Diagnosis.  The last sentence
should read:

Given the presence of left ventricular dysfunction, the
patient was started on a low-dose ACE inhibitor in the form
of lisinopril 7.5 mg p.o. q.d. to aid in cardiac remodeling.
As an outpatient, the patient may benefit from an increase in
the patient's Lopressor from 12.5 mg p.o. b.i.d. to 25 mg
p.o. b.i.d. to aid in the post myocardial infarction state.
Although, as mentioned earlier, this was attempted in house,
and had a profound affect on the patient's heart rate
(dropping into the low 50s).  This should be reattempted with
a goal heart rate of no less than 60.

Please amend Discharge Medications:  Lisinopril 7.5 mg p.o.
q.d.

Please amend Discharge Instructions: The patient was to
follow up with his primary care physician (Dr. Felicia Mendez)
in one to two weeks.

DISCHARGE DISPOSITION:  The patient will now be discharged to
rehabilitation to aid in bed transfer (as the patient's wife
is also elderly), and the burden of care would be
sufficiently decreased if the patient were to regain strength
and able to sufficiently perform basis activities of daily
living such as bed to chair transfer at rehabilitation prior
to discharge to home.




                            Sarah Thomas Robert Dennis, M.D.  26093

Dictated By:Campos
MEDQUIST36
